title: Francisco Garre-Frutos
text:
Website  Email  Twitter  Github  Bluesky



2021–Present | Ph.D. Experimental Psychology, University of Granada.

Ph.D. founded by an FPU grant awarded by the Spanish Ministry of Universities, supervised by Juan Lupiáñez and Miguel A. Vadillo. My research focuses on the study of the learning conditions of Value-Modulated Attentional Capture (VMAC). Specifically, this thesis aims to test the extent to which we can consider VMAC as an implicit psychological process.

2021–2022 | M.S. Cogntive and Behavioral Neuroscience, University of Granada.
2016–2020 | B.S. Psychology, University of Murcia







Franco-Martínez, A., Rey-Sáez, R., Adrián-Ventura, J., … Garre-Frutos, F., … Vadillo, M. A. (in press). Replicating the unconscious working memory effect: A multisite preregistered study. Neuroscience of Consciousness. https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niaf046.

Martínez-López, P., Vázquez-Millán, A., Garre-Frutos, F., & Luque., D. (in press). Assessing the Validity Evidence for Habit Measures Based on Time Pressure. Behavior Research Methods. https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-025-02865-2.

Garre-Frutos, F., Lupiáñez, J., & Vadillo, M. A. (2025). Value-modulated attentional capture depends on awareness. Psychonomic Bulletin & Review, 32, 3025–3040. https://doi.org/10.3758/s13423-025-02734-1.

González, F., Garre-Frutos, F., Hinojosa-Aguayo, I., & Hall, G. (2025). Extinction of outcome-specific Pavlovian-to-instrumental transfer (PIT), instrumental outcome devaluation, and reward-related attentional capture are predicted by affect-driven impulsivity. Learning & Behavior. https://doi.org/10.3758/s13420-025-00676-1.

Garre-Frutos, F., A. Ariza., & González, F. (2024). The effect of reward and punishment on the extinction of attentional capture elicited by value-related stimuli. Psychological Research, 89, 89. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00426-025-02115-2.

Garre-Frutos, F., Vadillo, M. A., González, F., & Lupiáñez, J. (2024). On the reliability of value-modulated attentional capture: An online replication and multiverse analysis. Behavior Research Methods, 56(6), 5986-6003. https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-023-02329-5.





Franco-Martínez, A., Ferrer-Mendieta, A., Peiro-Lanchares, C., Vadillo, M. A., & Garre-Frutos, F. (2025). Choose your own PAS? Quantitative and Qualitative Validity Evidence for the Perceptual Awareness Scale. PsyArXiv. https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/n7zuw_v1. Submitted manuscript.
Theeuwes, J., Manini, G., Garre-Frutos, F., Lupiañez, J., & Van Moorselaar, D. The capture debate revisited: Proactive or reactive suppression—That is the Question. Submitted manuscript.
Garre-Frutos, F., Vadillo, M. A. Theeuwes, J., Van Moorselaar, D., & Lupiañez, J. (2025). The role of selective attention in value-modulated attentional capture. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/kv2rx_v2. Submitted manuscript.

Manini, G.*, Garre-Frutos, F.*, Botta, F., Martínez-Arévalo, E., & Lupiañez, J. (2025). Facilitation by Irrelevant Distractors Under High Perceptual Load: Suppression or Controlled Attentional Capture? PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/xq2h8_v3. Submitted manuscript.

Manini, G., Garre-Frutos, F., Botta, F., Martínez-Arévalo, E., Lupiañez, J. (2024). Distraction vs. Interference: Handling fully irrelevant vs. potentially relevant distractors. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/rkpb8_v1. Submitted manuscript.

Garre-Frutos, F., Lupiañez, J. (2024). Inhibition of Return and Learned Value. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/p4m29. Submitted manuscript.





Garre-Frutos, F.*, López-Martínez, P*., Luque, D., Vadillo, M. A., González, F. (2024). Explicit knowledge modulates Pavlovian attentional biases. In preparation.

López-Martínez, P., Garre-Frutos, F., Vadillo, M. A. Luque, D. Lingering habits: Testing the temporal stability of habits. Data being collected.

López-Martínez, P., Garre-Frutos, F., Vadillo, M. A. Luque, D. Reliability and validity of experimental measures of habits. In preparation.

Garre-Frutos, F., Solana, P., López-Martínez, P., Lupiañez, J., González, F., & Vadillo, M. A. Is there value in value-driven distraction? A meta-analysis of studies on individual differences. In preparation.

Rey-Sáez, R., Franco-Martínez, A., Castillejo, A., Vadillo, M. A., & Garre-Frutos, F.. A multilevel signal detection approach to unconscious processing research. In preparation.
